body:
{
  "title": "Anchor All-Hands Q4 2026",
  "compiled_truth": "The Q4 2026 all-hands was held on January 3, 2027, kicking off the new year with a full review of the previous quarter and a forward-looking roadmap session. All eight members of [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) were present — a rare occurrence given that [Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) and [Ari Leclerc](people/ari-leclerc) had been traveling for customer onboarding through most of December.\n\n[Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) opened the meeting with a candid assessment of Q4. Revenue came in at roughly 87% of target, which she attributed to two delayed enterprise contracts that slipped into January. She was blunt about it: \"We scoped the deals correctly, but our procurement timelines were naive.\" That said, the technical milestones were largely hit. [Miro Bergmann](people/miro-bergmann) walked through the single-pass primitive benchmarks from the latest iteration, noting a 2.3x throughput improvement on streaming workloads compared to the August baseline. The room seemed genuinely energized by that number. Miro cautioned that edge cases in authentication handoff still needed attention, and [Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura) volunteered to pair on that work in the first two weeks of January.\n\nOn the go-to-market side, [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) presented a breakdown of the pipeline. There are now eleven active conversations with mid-market and enterprise prospects, up from four at the start of Q3. Dani flagged that three of these are in regulated industries — which means longer cycles but stickier contracts. [Nova Mensah](people/nova-mensah) raised a concern about documentation gaps that prospects keep hitting during technical evaluations. This sparked a twenty-minute discussion about whether to hire a dedicated devrel or redistribute the load internally. No decision was made, but Seren said she'd circulate a proposal by end of week.\n\n[Kael Petrov](people/kael-petrov) gave a brief infrastructure update — the staging environment had been migrated to a new provider, cutting CI pipeline times by about 40%. Paz followed up with notes from two on-site customer visits in December, highlighting that deterministic replay was the feature most frequently cited as a differentiator.\n\nOKRs for Q1 2027 were drafted live during the second half of the meeting. Key objectives include closing at least two of the slipped enterprise deals, shipping the v0.9 SDK with improved error semantics, and publishing a technical whitepaper co-authored by Miro and Tal. The meeting wrapped after about ninety minutes — longer than ususal but nobody seemed to mind.",
  "timeline": [
    "- **2026-10-01** | Q4 2026 begins; [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) sets quarterly revenue and shipping targets at prior all-hands.",
    "- **2026-10-18** | [Miro Bergmann](people/miro-bergmann) merges streaming throughput optimizations into main branch, beginning benchmark cycle.",
    "- **2026-11-09** | [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) opens first conversations with three regulated-industry prospects.",
    "- **2026-11-22** | [Kael Petrov](people/kael-petrov) completes staging environment migration, reducing CI times by ~40%.",
    "- **2026-12-05** | [Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) and [Ari Leclerc](people/ari-leclerc) begin December on-site customer visits.",
    "- **2026-12-19** | Two enterprise contracts expected to close in Q4 slip to January due to procurement delays.",
    "- **2027-01-03** | Q4 2026 all-hands held; full team present. Q1 2027 OKRs drafted.",
    "- **2027-01-10** | [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) circulates devrel hiring proposal to team for async feedback.",
    "- **2027-01-20** | [Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura) and Miro begin pairing on authentication handoff edge cases."
  ]
}
